rationale: Statistically overrepresented in LLM output; sounds performative rather than genuine. Human writers almost never start a sentence with 'delve into'.
AI cliché used to gesture at complexity; almost always hollow decoration rather than meaningful description.
Corporate buzzword that LLMs import from business-speak; 'use' is almost always clearer and more direct.
Filler hedge that delays the actual information; common AI padding phrase signalling something important without actually saying it.
Hyperbolic adjective used by LLMs to add apparent importance; rarely justified and quickly fatigues the reader.
Metaphor overused in AI-written surveys ('the AI landscape', 'the competitive landscape'); loses all imagery through repetition.
Tech-speak adjective that LLMs apply to almost everything; vague enough to mean nothing while sounding technical.
Urgency adjective that AI models insert reflexively to inflate importance; desensitises the reader when every step is 'crucial'.
Management-speak verb that LLMs favour for abstract nouns ('foster collaboration', 'foster growth'); 'build' or 'encourage' is usually more direct.
Quasi-poetic noun that AI models use to dress up mundane domains ('the realm of AI'); 'area' or 'field' is almost always cleaner.
